detection.rebuild_hint: If you made structural changes this session, call rebuild_index before your next sqry query.

tools.when: immediate callers of suspect function
immediate callees of suspect function
multi-level caller/callee chains when one-hop insufficient
check if bug site is in circular dependency
call path from entry point to bug site
blast radius — what else breaks if this symbol is wrong
jump to definition of symbol from stack traces
all usages of suspect symbol across codebase
structural call paths from input handlers to dangerous sinks (exec, eval, innerHTML, raw SQL)
full call tree from auth/authz entry points to verify coverage
circular dependencies that could cause infinite loops (DoS vectors)
dead code with old vulnerabilities or stale auth checks
high-complexity functions (cyclomatic >15) for manual security review
verify security-critical functions only called from trusted contexts
functions matching security patterns (auth*, sanitize*, validate*, escape*)
FFI/HTTP boundaries where trust assumptions change
cyclomatic complexity of changed files — flag regressions
check if changed symbols introduced or participate in cycles
downstream impact of changed public APIs
newly-dead code after refactors or API changes
structural changes between PR branch and base branch
verify callers of changed functions still work with new signature

retro.context: structural code quality analysis for retrospective

tools.when: structural changes between this week's HEAD and last week's tag/commit
complexity trends — adding or reducing complexity?
new cycles introduced this week
overall codebase health metrics for retrospective dashboard

plan-eng-review.context: structural architecture analysis for plan review

tools.when: visualize module dependencies to validate architecture boundaries
dependency neighborhood around components the plan modifies
dependency tree of modules the plan touches
existing cycles the plan should address or avoid
cross-language boundaries the plan must respect
no circular dependencies in shipped code
catch dead code being shipped
verify complexity hasn't regressed
